channel.title: Comments for page "Until Death"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Until Death"

item.content:encoded:
+500

This phenomenal tale deserves every single one of them. Congrats!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Stuff like this is a big part of why I love this site. It almost doesn't even contradict my headcanon because it's such a different interpretation of events, and really, there's no reason I can't have several different "maybes" floating around in my head at the same time. Maybe 106 has always been a monster, and enjoys nothing more than causing human suffering, and maybe it found its way into humanity during WWI, but then maybe it's just poor old Dr. Scranton, trying to find his way back to his wife, his time in that space inbetween spaces having turned him into a monster… and maybe he doesn't even know it.

Anyway, I love the new twist on old classics. Thank you for writing it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When I realized who you were casting as 106, ugh, I hated it. I hate that you made poor Scranton into 106. I hate it so much that I have to upvote it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 106 tales are amazing to me. If done well, they are some of the most terrifying stories I've read. The sheer panic and confusion of such a horrific entity is just nightmare fuel. 3001 is my favorite SCP ever, hands down. I absolutely adore it. I can't explain why, I just do. So take one of my favorite SCPs for tales, and give me a plot twist that directly links to my favorite SCP. I will admit it here and now, my eyes watered a little when I read "Red" and "Anna" and realizing it was Scranton. I didn't cry, but…damn, if it didn't get me close. This is a new favorite of mine. Take my +1, you magnificent bastard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Usually I really don't like SCPs or Tales that dictate the origin or final fate of *other* SCPs, technologies, characters, etc.
But something about this - about The Old Man actually being poor Scranton - just really fucking clicks for me. It's not necessarily the headcanon I'm going to insist on, as something about the Old Man's vagueness and multiple interpretations is just fine, but goddamn if I don't like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: HOLY FREAKING MOLY. Anyway, this seems like a fitting backstory for 106 and why he's been contained

item.title: This story is wonderful!
item.content:encoded:
I was deeply shocked by it
—The old man are romantic ,doesn't he —

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Amazing writing so +1'd.

But really meh origin story for the Old Man. Theres no real backstory connection in there that links the two in a satisfying way, I mean you can even turn 106 into 682 if you had to to make this concept work.

I'm sorry but I really can't accept this into my personal headcanon. I'd love to, but Dr. Scranton's finality needs to be special, like doomed to be in the red dimension for eternity and have a lonely death. His purpose and story just goes out of focus and 3001 just becomes an anecdote when you tack him to the Old Man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, this is what happened to Dr. Scranton… You either die a scientist, or live long enough to see yourself become a slimy old boogieman. +1

item.title: Error +404
item.content:encoded: Wow, what a wild ride. Top notch twist you got right here.

item.title: Damn you
item.content:encoded: Take my +1 I hate you but at least it endsmy suffering on what happened to Scranton, wish I had found this earlier.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
OK, the end of 3001 was heart breaking, truly, but I thought at least it was a form of closure for Anna, and as peaceful and as happy an end as Scranton could have hoped for..but no, not only has he possibly never realised how he nearly broke through from the void to see Anna again, however temporarily, he remained in the torture of the void for DECADES more, until somehow finding a way to transmute into a form which allows him to access reality again - a form so hideous it's notorious even in the Foundation, a form in which his desperate attempt to reunite with his lost love only results in her terror-filled escape attempt and subsequent horrible death.

This is the most horrible piece of writing I have ever seen on this site.

This is one of the most affecting, brilliant things here.

Headcannoned, hard. I think this could be a cognitohazard; it's really messed with my brain [argh Scranton is 106…wut?…nooooooo…..]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Admittedly have not read the entire article upon commenting this.

Close to, she could see the surface of the wall had softened, sagging in wet bulges. She hesitated.

I actually like how it sounds as a type of abbreviation, but it's also awkward.

item.title: Wow
item.content:encoded: Great tale, I love how it connected 3001 and 106. headcannoned and +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wasn’t a huge fan of 106 before- not that it isn’t great, I could never write sth like that! Just not very fond of blood and scary images. But this article sure added sth to it! So +1

item.title: Re: whoaaah
item.content:encoded: Same dude holy fish, not Anna! This is too much heartbreak and suffering!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
HOLY SHIT.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The connection with 3001 was made amazingly. The whole concept is sickening.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: oh ok thc u

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My personal handwave for essentially all of this is extrapolation:

"By the end of SCP-3001" Scranton has been in red reality for between 5 and 6 years. That's how long it took for him to get the stage he gets to there - sure, he can probably still distinguish emotions, and remembers what having a normal physical body was like.

By the start of this tale, Scranton has been in that reality for more than 30 years.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait, what?
I have to read 3001 again. I swear, I'm pretty sure you missed something…
EDIT: Yeah, all that was left was organs, one leg, and one arm. Whatever is missing in the Red Reality is certainly not enough to rebuild a whole human out of. Why is it corrosive, again? Also, Scranton is apparently missing MOST of his tongue, let alone extra of it.
DOUBLE EDIT: Disregard everything I said, I was just being a dumb stupid idiot. But again, why the corrosion, and why the extra tongue?
THE THIRD EYE: I'm pretty sure Scranton, mad as he may have been by the end of 3001, could still distinguish between emotions. Like, he still knows what "rape" is, riiiight? He knew how Anna was reacting to being melted alive, as well, riiiiight?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Anna and Scranton really can't catch a break, can they?
Time to throw an acidic party!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i ask how scantron escaped every day, but I think you're talking about "scranton"

item.title: whoaaah
item.content:encoded:
Anna…
I loved it really, but man, no happy ending for my favorite researcher, my dude Scranton?
It had me scraming to the screen in awe.
Upvoted so hard.
But…I wont keep it as headcannon, I am still waiting for a not so bitter ending. bittersweet indeed, but my main guy Scranton deserves better.
+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll take "Articles I Thought I Upvoted Already" for 400, Alex.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not really my headcanon (I still prefer The Young Man), but the writing is still excellent vivid. I find it really hard to get unnerved by written stories, but this one has probably come the closest to actually frightening me. The imagery it paints becomes so clear in my mind. Excellent writing!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this tale because of the questions it poses. How did Scantron escape? What made him into 106? What are his goals? These questions both make this tale - and SCP-106 itself - even more terrifying.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My fovorite article! Nice work!
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's great, and ohgodthedetails. +1.

However, I don't headcanon this tale. -1 on me for being too rigid to accept this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You clever son of a bitch.

At first I though this was going to just be a regular story about the first encounter with 106, I wasn't expecting anything out of it and was just content with it being well writen.

Then you made the best crosslink I've seen in a while.

If only I could +1 more than once, you magnificent bastard.

item.title: Re: A New Word for This
item.content:encoded: I think Ensophos means that this tale was so good, it basicly forced itself to be their new headcanon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fuck.

This was terrifying on it’s own and then the ending really transcends to a level of horror that enhances both the articles this story references.

Oh god now I’m realizing WHY she knew about dimensional anomalies and you’re really a bastard!

+1, you wonderful asshole

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh my, I also had the idea of writing a disturbing 106 love story. Looks like you beat me to it. Excellent and well written!

item.title: Re: A New Word for This
item.content:encoded: It doesn't really force you to accept it as headcanon, it provides a new view, possibility, perhaps, for a story, but you can believe whatever your heart desires.

item.title: A New Word for This
item.content:encoded:
I really wanted to believe that The Young Man was the origin story of 106. The uniform jacket, the smile, the glinting eyes, the militaristic feel of his SCP:CB theme Bump in the Night… all of these reinforced my headcanon that 106 was Corporal Lawrence.

And then I read this.

I came up with a new word to describe stories like this:

Headcrabbon: Any narrative set within an established universe that violently forces the reader/viewer to accept its canon as headcanon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think ngcq1811 means that SCP-106/Robert prefers dragging victims between the ages 10-25 to his pocket dimension because he wants to treat some of the youngest ones as his children and create a family with Anna, since reproducing with her is off the table for obvious reasons.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Those are the names Robert Scranton chose for the child he’d never be able to have. He picked Lucy first, then picked David with red’s “help”.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: David and Lucy? I’m afraid I don’t understand what you mean.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So 106 was looking for David and Lucy this whole time, hence the 10-25 preferred age bracket…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: *screams uncontrollably* I don't know what terrifies me more, the amazing writing or how well it fits. This is amazing but also horrible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Some parts of the article do not sound like 106 to me. Like it using the parts of others to repair itself. It also conflicts with my headcanon for it to be 106 but it's so well written and I found a way to incorporate it based on the differences.

Anyway, good article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
NO NO NO NO NO NO WHY

Just take this +1 and leave forever.

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded:
Well it's the first tale I've written that has made it to +100, so by definition it's the fastest. It's also by far my most popular in terms of combining overall score and upvote/downvote ratio. I am certainly not complaining!

Thank you to everyone who voted. You are responsible for encouraging me to try other things very much like this again ;)

item.title: Re: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded: Thanks very much TheeSherm (and YossiLeiner too) - I really like the reading. I will heartily endorse the playlist, and encourage everyone to give some of the videos a listen!

I'm sincerely surprised that it took this long. This is an amazing tale, and it deserves every single upvote twice over.

Never do anything like this ever again though.

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
You've got a big fan in YossiLeiner, he's been hitting me up to do this one for a looooong time.

Meanwhile, I love this twist. I enjoy the tragic backstory enough to keep this as a headcanon.

https://youtu.be/S3MtyVqNz8I

item.title: Re: Holy Shit
item.content:encoded:
I can't believe I missed the Site-120 and the date on the link

I hid the researcher's name the whole time to avoid spoilers (which made for some really unwieldy sentences), but I took a punt that no-one would pick up on Site-120. Thankfully that seems to have been the case.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nooooooo
NOOOOOOOOO
WHY DID YOU HAVE TO DO THIS TO MY HEART
take my +1 you sick fuck

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absolutely fantastic. You have a great talent for vivid description. Excellent pacing, pithy characterisation, and built around such a stomach-curdling, narratively plausible crosslink. Deserves an even higher rating than it currently holds.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I translated your article into Chinese, and I also the translator of SCP-3001.
When I done that, I asked myself: OK, then, how should it end? Take my upvote because I like BAD END.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Son of a bitch, man. I always worried that a humanizing story to 106 would somewhat kill the horror of it, but this does the opposite and skyrockets it to the Class-C "Broken Entry" Wormhole it originated from.

Well done, man. Too bad I'm limited to only one +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh. My god. That was fucked up. Headcanoned. So much.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Holy fuck. Holy fucking shit.

This has gotta be one of my favourite tales ever. I got here via knowledge of a continuation of SCP-3001's story but I had no fucking clue this intertwined with 106.

Holy shit, I'm in shock.

This is a legendary tale and needs more attention.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yargh… This is horrifying. And that crosslink twist…

+ show block
– hide block

That Dr. Scranton is SCP-106, mutated and insane, is even more horrifying. It pulls the pity string, too. The guy's become a lunatic monster. He was a scientist, most likely a brilliant one, given the fact that the Scranton Reality Anchor was named after him. (I presume he played a fairly critical role in the invention of that device), and now he's reduced almost to an animal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm both scared and fascinated.

+1

Edit: DDDDD:::::

I think that's probably the quickest one of my tales has hit the half-century :)

I should have noted in my original author post that the actual point of inspiration for this was the predatory teapot in SCP-2649. Thanks, GeometryPrime!

item.title: that ending ...
item.content:encoded:
Here : "+1". This is one of my upvotes, and I give it to you. Take it. You clever writer. Take it.

Headcanon for me.
Did not see that coming.
Take the upvote and get it away from me as far as possible. Loved the twist at the end.
